Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: By integrating geometric subspace diagnostics with deterministic optimization proxies, we can develop a curvature-aware precision routing framework that improves the efficiency and accuracy of machine learning models, particularly in resource-constrained environments.
Related Work: Existing work on precision routing and model optimization has focused on heuristic approaches, whereas our proposal emphasizes a rigorous, curvature-aware framework that tightly couples geometric diagnostics with deterministic optimization. This distinction enables our approach to provide formal guarantees on numerical stability and computational overhead, setting it apart from prior efforts.
Abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for machine learning, leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies to improve model efficiency and accuracy. Our approach prioritizes mathematical transparency, numerical stability, and computational overhead bounds, making it suitable for resource-constrained environments. By integrating spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, we establish a feasible pipeline for exploratory single-GPU validation. Our framework operationalizes a training-free routing protocol, replacing heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework on benchmark datasets, comparing its performance to existing precision routing methods.
Investigate the impact of geometric subspace diagnostics on model accuracy and efficiency, using techniques such as gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning analysis.

Assess the effectiveness of deterministic optimization proxies in improving model stability and generalization, using metrics such as PAC-Bayes generalization bounds and explicit condition number constraints.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require significant computational resources for large-scale models, potentially limiting its applicability in extremely resource-constrained environments.

The effectiveness of the curvature-aware precision routing framework may depend on the quality of the geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, which may require careful tuning and validation.
